taxonomy: Phalangium squameum is a species within the arachnid family, specifically within the infraorder Opiliones. It belongs to the order Araneomorphae, but it's worth noting that Opiliones is sometimes classified as an arachnid order separate from Araneomorphae. This classification is part of a broader system of arachnid classification. The infraorder Opiliones is further divided into several subinfraorders, with Phalangium squameum being part of the subinfraorder Eupnoi. Its classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the arachnid order Opiliones.
